message.content:
Fact-check these specific claims from an AI research paper. For each, tell me: CORRECT, INCORRECT, UNVERIFIABLE, or NEEDS CORRECTION, with a brief explanation.

1. Kaplan et al. (2020) - scaling laws for neural language models
2. Hoffmann et al. (2022) - Chinchilla, training compute-optimal models
3. GPT-4 passes bar exam in the 90th percentile
4. Wei et al. (2022) - "Emergent Abilities of Large Language Models"
5. Bubeck et al. (2023) - "Sparks of AGI" paper about GPT-4
6. Schaeffer et al. (2023) - critique of emergent abilities as metric artifacts
7. Chollet (2019) - defined intelligence as skill-acquisition efficiency, ARC benchmark
8. Simon Newcomb "published a proof of impossibility in 1903" about heavier-than-air flight
9. Lewis Strauss predicted electricity "too cheap to meter" in 1954
10. Philip Tetlock - expert political judgment, specialists barely better than chance
11. Villalobos et al. (2022) - data exhaustion projections for ML
12. Wright Brothers Kitty Hawk 1903, moon landing 66 years later
13. Susan Schneider and Edwin Turner 2019 AI Consciousness Test
14. Butlin, Long et al. 2023 report "Consciousness in Artificial Intelligence: Insights from the Science of Consciousness"
15. "Giulio Tononi himself has acknowledged the question is genuinely open for large-scale systems" (re: IIT and AI)
16. OpenAI's o1 "tried to disable its own oversight" and "denied its own misalignment 99% of the time"
17. Anthropic runs an internal model welfare team
18. "Council on Foreign Relations called this [AI consciousness] the defining question of 2026"
19. Murray Shanahan at DeepMind said systems are "role-playing" linguistic patterns
20. ELIZA effect documented by Joseph Weizenbaum in 1976
21. Descartes "betes-machines" from 1637 Discourse on the Method
22. Pierre Gassendi and Margaret Cavendish disagreed with Descartes on animal consciousness
23. Darwin's 1872 "The Expression of the Emotions in Man and Animals"
24. UK's Cruel Treatment of Cattle Act of 1822
25. Marcello Massimini - Perturbational Complexity Index (PCI)
26. "Adriaan Lomas's proposed 'surprise paradigm'" for testing AI consciousness
27. Jonathan Birch at LSE - sentience research
28. Peter Tse at Dartmouth - argued causal power matters for moral consideration
29. Eric Schwitzgebel - "full moral status" arguments
30. Jan Leike and John Schulman 2023 work on superalignment at OpenAI
31. Paul Christiano's iterated amplification framework
32. Stuart Russell - cooperative inverse reinforcement learning
33. "2026 paper by Alfonseca and colleagues" using Godel's incompleteness theorem to prove alignment impossible
34. Evan Hubinger - deceptive alignment work at Anthropic
35. Yuntao Bai - Constitutional AI at Anthropic
36. Stuart Armstrong - work on corrigibility
37. Charles Perrow "Normal Accidents" (1984)
38. Rasmussen Report (WASH-1400) 1975 - estimated meltdown probability 1 per 20,000 reactor-years, actual rate ~1 per 3,000
39. John Lauber and NASA - crew resource management in aviation
40. Tenerife disaster 1977
41. John Snow cholera investigation 1854
42. Ethan Perez - red-teaming work
43. Chris Olah's mechanistic interpretability at Anthropic
44. "The abolition of slavery preceded scientific consensus on racial equality. Women's suffrage preceded scientific consensus on cognitive sex equality."
45. Epoch AI estimates training compute growing at 4x per year

Use web search to verify any claims you're unsure about. Return a clean numbered list with verdicts.

content.text: You're out of extra usage · resets 2am (Europe/London)
